title: JUnit tests

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for JUnit tests; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: JUnit tests arrange isolated state, perform one observable behavior, and assert normal, boundary, and failure contracts with deterministic fixtures and readable parameterization.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A parameterized test covers empty, singleton, normal, and boundary inputs, while assertThrows checks the exact invalid-input contract. Your goal is to create a deterministic unit test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Exceptions, resources and I/O; Lambdas, streams and optionals; Maven project lifecycle; Exception flow and stack traces. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: JUnit tests arrange isolated state, perform one observable behavior, and assert normal, boundary, and failure contracts with deterministic fixtures and readable parameterization. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Test order is not a state-sharing mechanism, sleeps and real external systems create flakiness, mocks can overfit implementation, and line coverage does not prove assertions or edge cases. The learner must reject this documented misconception: A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.

includes: Write isolated assertions, parameterized cases and exception tests with readable fixtures.
Create a deterministic unit test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.
excludes: Test order is not a state-sharing mechanism, sleeps and real external systems create flakiness, mocks can overfit implementation, and line coverage does not prove assertions or edge cases.

outcomes: Create a deterministic unit test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.

examples.title: JUnit tests: direct language case
examples.situation: A parameterized test covers empty, singleton, normal, and boundary inputs, while assertThrows checks the exact invalid-input contract.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A parameterized test covers empty, singleton, normal, and boundary inputs, while assertThrows checks the exact invalid-input contract.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.

examples.title: JUnit tests: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A repository dependency is replaced at its external boundary, but the test asserts service output rather than private helper call order.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A repository dependency is replaced at its external boundary, but the test asserts service output rather than private helper call order.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Test order is not a state-sharing mechanism, sleeps and real external systems create flakiness, mocks can overfit implementation, and line coverage does not prove assertions or edge cases.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Create a deterministic unit test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.
misconceptions.correction: Derive JUnit cases and assertions from the public contract, isolate each fixture, and cover normal, boundary, and failure behavior deterministically.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.

analogy.example: a versioned delivery line whose public labels, recipe, inspections, and investigation record make one artifact reproducible can introduce JUnit tests, after the canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: packages, Maven, JUnit, debugging, and project evidence
limitations: access control is not a security boundary and caches, plugins, bytecode, secrets, and platform differences need technical controls

recap.summary: You should now be able to create a deterministic unit test suite covering normal, boundary and failure behavior. Keep this boundary in view: Test order is not a state-sharing mechanism, sleeps and real external systems create flakiness, mocks can overfit implementation, and line coverage does not prove assertions or edge cases.
retrievalPrompts: Explain JUnit tests using canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate terms and one observable consequence.
Why is this belief incorrect: A green test with high line coverage proves the implementation is correct and makes boundary analysis unnecessary.
Predict this case from memory: A repository dependency is replaced at its external boundary, but the test asserts service output rather than private helper call order.
